title: Macroeconomic Nowcasting and Forecasting with Big Data

source.name: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

summary: This New York Fed staff report reviews the evolution of macroeconomic tracking methods using big data and details the econometric methodology behind the New York Fed Staff Nowcast system. However, the crawled text captures only a brief abstract — the full paper content (PDF) was not retrieved during crawling.
key_points: Authors: Brandyn Bok, Daniele Caratelli, Domenico Giannone, Argia M. Sbordone, and Andrea Tambalotti
Published as New York Fed Staff Report Number 830, November 2017
JEL classification: C32, C53, C55, E32
Describes the methodology behind the New York Fed Staff Nowcast for estimating GDP growth in real-time
Covers evolution of big data handling techniques in macroeconomics and econometric advances
Full paper available only in PDF format; crawled text is thin and lacks substantive content
why_it_matters: The thin crawled text provides minimal analytical value for a finance research archive. The actual methodology content resides in a PDF that was not captured. For research on nowcasting methodology, the full PDF should be retrieved.
macro_context: The source describes historical development of nowcasting techniques (2017) but provides no current macro data, forecasts, or catalysts. It is a methodology paper, not a source of macro intelligence.
